lesson.title: CAPS.PhysicalSciences.Gr10.PHY.2: Gravitational potential energy (Ep = mgh). Kinetic energy...
lesson.explanation: Kinematics describes motion without considering forces. Key concepts: displacement (change in position, can be negative), velocity (rate of change of displacement), acceleration (rate of change of velocity). Equations of motion: v = u + at, s = ut + ½at², v² = u² + 2as. Motion graphs: x-t graphs show position over time (gradient = velocity), v-t graphs show velocity (gradient = acceleration, area = displacement).

practice_problems.question: Calculate the gravitational potential energy of a 5 kg rock at a height of 12 m above the ground. (g = 9.8 m/s^2)
hints: Substitute m = 5 kg, g = 9.8 m/s^2, h = 12 m.

practice_problems.question: A 2 kg ball is thrown upward at 10 m/s. Calculate its kinetic energy at the moment of release.
hints: Substitute m = 2 kg and v = 10 m/s.

practice_problems.question: A 0.5 kg ball is dropped from 20 m. Using conservation of energy (no air resistance), find its speed just before hitting the ground. (g = 10 m/s^2)
hints: At the top: Ep = mgh, Ek = 0. At the bottom: Ep = 0, Ek = 1/2 mv^2.
Set mgh = 1/2 mv^2. Notice that mass cancels.

practice_problems.question: A force of 50 N pushes a box 8 m along a floor at an angle of 30 degrees to the horizontal. Calculate the work done by this force.
practice_problems.explanation: W = Fd cos theta = (50)(8)(cos 30) = 400 x 0.866 = 346.4 J. Only the horizontal component of force does work along the displacement.

practice_problems.question: A roller coaster car (mass 500 kg) is at the top of a 30 m hill moving at 5 m/s. Calculate its speed at the bottom of the hill, assuming no friction. (g = 10 m/s^2)
hints: Total energy at top = total energy at bottom (conservation of mechanical energy).
Ep(top) + Ek(top) = Ep(bottom) + Ek(bottom), with h = 0 at the bottom.
What concept from Energy applies here?
Apply the method for Energy step by step.
Start by writing down what you know about Energy.

misconceptions.why_students_think_this: This mistake is common because students often skip a key step.

assessment.title: Assessment: Gravitational potential energy (Ep = mgh). Kinetic energy (Ek = ½mv²). Mechanical energy. Conservation of mechanical energy (non-conservative forces absent). Work done by a force (W = FΔx cos θ)
